svg.text: Animated visualization — watch the pointers move through Rails → React → Browser

g.text: ⏳ Page is interactive! Waiting for slow data...
Data streamed from Rails via async props
Helper starts streaming response to browser immediately
props: { title: "Header" } sent to React renderer — no waiting
React: Renders page with Suspense fallback
Header visible immediately, "Loading..." placeholder for slow data
Rails: sleep 2 executes (simulates slow DB query)
Page is INTERACTIVE while Rails processes slow data!
Slow prop streamed to browser, Promise resolves
React: Suspense fallback replaced with real content
During Step 4, the page is fully interactive! Users can scroll, click, and use the header while Rails processes the slow query.
This is the power of async props — no blocking, no full-page spinners, just progressive enhancement.
